executiveSummary.text: Regime shifted to cautious risk-off after the prior AI-led melt-up: S&P 500 -0.74%, Nasdaq -0.89%, Dow -1.21%, Russell 2000 -1.31%, VIX 16.06 (+1.8%). The driver was not a growth scare alone; oil and yields rose together as Middle East risk re-priced inflation and Fed optionality.

Rates/USD are the main transmission channel: 10Y 4.49%, 30Y 4.99%, 5Y 4.21%, DXY 99.55 (+0.33%). Reuters reported the Fed Beige Book showed consumers and businesses squeezed by inflation, while ADP private payrolls rose 122k in May; this keeps a June Fed hold/high-for-longer base case alive.

AI/semis stayed bifurcated: NVDA -3.6%, MSFT -3.2% and TSM -2.2% lagged, while KLAC +3.9%, Shin-Etsu +4.5%, AMAT +2.2%, LRCX +2.8% showed equipment/materials scarcity is still being rewarded. This supports KL's 'own what AI depends on' thesis, but warns against assuming all AI beta rises together.

Scarcity assets corrected hard despite oil strength: silver -2.6%, copper -2.5%, COPX -3.6%, uranium ETFs URA -5.7%/URNM -5.9%, while gold slipped only -0.3%. This looks like real-rate/USD/liquidity pressure plus crowded-position de-risking, not a clean collapse in the long-term electrification/energy-bottleneck thesis.

Singapore remains a relative defensive winner: STI +1.18% by Yahoo data; Business Times reported STI +0.8% with DBS and OCBC at fresh highs as banks led gains. Positive for KL's SG dividend sleeve, but higher global yields remain a headwind for S-REIT re-rating.

Scenario weight: 55% choppy risk-on with rates as ceiling; 25% oil/rates shock causing 5-10% equity drawdown; 20% disinflation/liquidity relief restarting broad risk-on. Confidence: medium, because geopolitics can change the oil-yield path quickly.

regime.label: Cautious risk-off: oil/yields up, AI leadership narrower

usGlobal.what: US equities fell with weaker breadth: S&P, Nasdaq, Dow and small caps all down; VIX only modestly higher.
usGlobal.why: CNBC and Reuters linked the move to renewed US-Iran/Middle East tension, higher oil and Treasury yields. A low-but-rising VIX says orderly de-risking, not panic.
usGlobal.implication: Do not chase weakness indiscriminately; watch whether VIX moves above 20 and small caps/credit confirm stress.

ratesMacro.what: Treasury yields and USD rose together.
ratesMacro.why: ADP private payrolls +122k and ISM services at 53.6 were firm enough to resist cut pricing, while Reuters Fed Beige Book flagged inflation strain. Cleveland Fed Hammack also recently warned rates may need to rise if inflation does not abate.
ratesMacro.implication: Higher real/nominal yields cap long-duration tech, REITs and metals even if the AI capex story remains intact.

asiaSingapore.why: Business Times reported STI +0.8% on Jun 3 with DBS and OCBC at fresh highs; Yahoo data also showed STI positive. Higher-for-longer rates support bank NIM/ROE narratives but weigh on REIT discount rates.
asiaSingapore.implication: KL's SG dividend banks remain ballast; avoid assuming REITs will rally until global yields fall.

commodities.what: Oil rose while metals and uranium sold off.
commodities.why: CNBC/Reuters reported oil climbed as Iran tensions and Hormuz/shipping risk returned; silver/copper/uranium fell as USD and yields rose and crowded scarcity trades de-risked.
commodities.implication: This is stagflationary cross-asset pressure: oil hurts margins/inflation expectations; metals can fall if liquidity/real rates tighten despite long-run scarcity.

geopolitics.what: Middle East risk is again market-relevant through energy, shipping, inflation expectations and rates.
geopolitics.why: Reuters reported world shares dropped and oil climbed as hopes for a swift end to the Iran war faded; CNBC cited renewed US/Iran/Israel escalation risk.
geopolitics.implication: Transmission path is oil -> inflation expectations -> Fed hold/hike risk -> higher real yields/USD -> pressure on equities, REITs and metals.

moves.what: URNM -5.94% and URA -5.67%; uranium sleeve abnormal drawdown.
moves.why: Commodity/scarcity complex de-risked as USD/yields rose; no thesis-breaking nuclear-demand headline found in last-24h scan.
moves.implication: Positioning/liquidity shock more than demand thesis damage. 2nd order: if oil/geopolitics keeps rates high, uranium equities can lag even with supportive nuclear fundamentals.
moves.action: HOLD / WATCH: add only on stabilization after another 10-15% drawdown or clear utility contracting/supply catalyst; invalidator = reactor-policy reversal or sustained uranium spot weakness.

moves.why: Higher DXY and yields pressured cyclicals/metals; crowded scarcity trades were sold while oil rose.
moves.implication: Long-term grid/AI/electrification scarcity thesis intact, but tactical entry should respect liquidity. 3rd order: if energy inflation delays Fed cuts, financing costs can delay marginal mining supply and capex projects even as demand thesis persists.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only if copper stabilizes with DXY/yields flat or falling. Invalidate tactical add if copper breaks lower on China demand deterioration, not just USD pressure.

moves.what: NVDA -3.62% and MSFT -3.17%; large AI bellwethers underperformed.
moves.why: Risk-off and duration pressure hit mega-cap AI; no last-24h thesis-breaking earnings/guidance item found. KL is deliberately NVDA-underweight, reducing regret risk.
moves.implication: AI leadership is narrowing/rotating. 2nd order: if hyperscaler capex remains strong, weakness in MSFT/NVDA can still feed equipment/foundry winners; if capex discipline appears, the whole AI chain reprices.
moves.action: HOLD / IGNORE for NVDA underweight; WATCH MSFT near further 5-8% pullback for quality add if cloud/AI revenue remains intact.

moves.what: KLAC +3.91% and Shin-Etsu/4063 +4.46% outperformed; AMAT +2.19%, LRCX +2.78%.
moves.why: Equipment/materials scarcity continued to attract capital even as broad tech fell; market is distinguishing semicap bottlenecks from generic AI beta.
moves.implication: Positive for KL's semicap sleeve and scarcity thesis. 2nd order: if foundry/HBM/advanced packaging capacity remains constrained, inspection, deposition/etch and materials suppliers can keep pricing power.
moves.action: HOLD; ADD only on broad-market pullbacks, not after vertical single-day moves. Invalidate if order books/capex guides roll over across TSM/hyperscalers.

moves.what: META +4.24% was the notable positive big-tech outlier.
moves.why: Likely rotation/company-specific AI monetization optimism; no fresh thesis-breaking item found in last-24h scan.
moves.implication: Helps KL big-tech sleeve diversification; do not extrapolate to all hyperscalers while MSFT/AMZN/GOOG lag.
moves.action: HOLD / IGNORE unless valuation outruns advertising and AI-product revenue growth.

thesisImpact.what: AI scarcity thesis intact but more selective.
thesisImpact.why: Equipment/materials outperformed while some hyperscalers/accelerator names lagged.
thesisImpact.implication: Prioritize bottleneck suppliers and cash-flow discipline over generic AI beta.
thesisImpact.action: HOLD core; rebalance only if one sleeve dominates risk budget.

narratives.title: Oil-yield feedback loop replaces simple safe-haven playbook
narratives.description: Middle East escalation lifted oil; oil can lift inflation expectations and term premium; that raises real/nominal yields and USD, which can pressure both equities and metals. This is why gold did not mechanically surge and copper/silver fell.
narratives.priceImpact: Negative for duration and leveraged risk; mixed for commodities depending on USD/real rates.

narratives.title: AI supply chain bifurcation
narratives.description: Market rewarded equipment/material bottlenecks but sold parts of mega-cap AI. The question is shifting from “is AI real?” to “who has pricing power after capex expectations are embedded?”
narratives.source: Yahoo price scan; Reuters tech scan background
narratives.priceImpact: Positive for bottlenecks; volatile for crowded AI winners.

narratives.title: Singapore banks as defensive yield/equity hybrid
narratives.description: DBS and OCBC fresh highs per Business Times show local banks still attract capital when rates stay high. REITs remain the offset because discount rates and refinancing costs rise.
narratives.priceImpact: Positive banks, neutral-to-negative REITs until yields fall.

secondOrderEffects.text: Oil shock -> inflation expectations -> Fed hold/hike optionality -> higher yields/USD -> lower valuation multiples for tech/REITs and forced selling in metals despite scarcity.
secondOrderEffects.implication: Do not read commodity weakness as automatic thesis failure; separate demand impairment from liquidity/real-rate pressure.

secondOrderEffects.text: Semicap outperformance while hyperscalers lag -> market may be pricing scarce inputs over end-platform margins.
secondOrderEffects.implication: KL should keep semicap/foundry/equipment sleeve, but avoid adding after momentum spikes unless broad market gives a discount.

secondOrderEffects.text: Singapore banks benefiting from higher rates can cushion local portfolio income, but the same rate path slows REIT recovery.
secondOrderEffects.implication: SG dividend sleeve remains useful ballast, yet a bank-heavy STI creates concentration risk if credit costs rise.

investment.thesis: Keep the AI bottleneck basket: ASML/LRCX/AMAT/KLAC/TSM/SNPS/Shin-Etsu plus grid/copper/uranium as long-term scarcity assets.
investment.trigger: Add in tranches only on 8-12% sleeve drawdowns or after confirmed capex/order strength with valuation reset.
investment.whyNow: Market is proving scarcity leadership is selective, not dead.
investment.invalidator: Hyperscaler capex cuts, foundry utilization rollover, or HBM/advanced-node capacity glut.

investment.thesis: Maintain SG bank ballast but do not chase fresh highs aggressively.
investment.trigger: Reinvest dividends or add only on broad STI/bank pullbacks >5-8% unless earnings/dividend guidance improves materially.
investment.whyNow: Higher rates support banks while global volatility favors defensives.
investment.invalidator: Rising credit costs, NIM compression without fee-income offset, or regulatory capital-return constraints.

trading.thesis: Use cash dry powder only if VIX/rates create dislocations, not on first red day.
trading.trigger: VIX >20 plus S&P down 5% from recent high while 10Y stops rising.
trading.whyNow: Current selloff is orderly and rate-driven; patience has option value.
trading.invalidator: Oil spike continues and 10Y breaks materially above 4.6-4.7%.

trading.trigger: DXY rolls over and copper/URNM stabilizes for 2-3 sessions after abnormal selloff.
trading.whyNow: Scarcity ETFs were hit by liquidity/positioning; mean reversion possible if rates calm.
trading.invalidator: China demand deterioration or uranium spot/contract weakness.

watchlist.thesis: Fed path is the key macro switch.
watchlist.trigger: CPI/PPI and FOMC June 16-17; watch whether market prices hikes rather than just no cuts.
watchlist.whyNow: Beige Book + oil makes inflation risk two-sided again.

watchlist.trigger: Brent sustainably above $100-105 or verified shipping/production disruption.
watchlist.whyNow: This would transmit directly into inflation expectations and yields.
watchlist.invalidator: Credible ceasefire, inventories rebuild, or shipping normalization.

riskWatch.name: Oil/rates stagflation impulse
riskWatch.description: Oil near high-$90s plus rising yields/DXY pressures margins, consumers, tech multiples and REITs.
riskWatch.mitigation: Keep cash ballast; add risk only after yields stabilize.

riskWatch.name: AI crowding and leadership narrowing
riskWatch.description: Mega-cap AI weakness alongside semicap strength means factor risk is high and broad AI beta can disappoint.
riskWatch.mitigation: Hold diversified AI stack; avoid single-name concentration; respect valuation.

riskWatch.name: Singapore bank concentration / REIT rate sensitivity
riskWatch.description: SG banks at fresh highs help portfolio income but STI can become bank-heavy; REITs remain vulnerable to higher long yields.
riskWatch.mitigation: Do not chase banks; stagger REIT adds only when yields fall or refinancing outlook improves.

keyEvents.text: Reuters: world shares dropped and oil climbed as hopes for swift end to Iran war faded.
Reuters: Fed Beige Book showed US consumers/businesses squeezed by inflation; ADP private payrolls rose 122k in May.
CNBC: 10Y Treasury yield neared 4.5% after jobs/services data and higher oil; ISM services 53.6.
Business Times: STI ended higher on Jun 3; DBS and OCBC hit fresh highs as banks led gains.

bottomLine.whatMatters: The day is about the oil -> inflation -> yields/USD chain, not just equity weakness. AI scarcity remains valid but narrower; Singapore banks are ballast; metals/uranium suffered liquidity/rate pressure.
bottomLine.whatToDo: Hold core portfolio. Keep cash as option value. Add only on defined dislocations: VIX >20 with yields stabilizing, semicap pullbacks with order strength intact, or metals/uranium stabilization after USD/yield relief.
bottomLine.whatToIgnore: Do not overreact to one-day NVDA/MSFT weakness or uranium/metals drawdowns as thesis failure; also do not chase Singapore banks simply because they printed fresh highs.
